book_summary: Leviticus has chapters 1-26 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book now covers offerings and reparation, priestly ordination and rupture, clean/unclean ritual states, body and illness safeguards, Day of Atonement, holiness and social justice, difficult sexual/penalty texts with anti-weaponization metadata, priestly holy-food and bodily-wholeness boundaries, the appointed-times calendar, continual lamp and bread of presence, proportional justice, Jubilee economics, and covenant blessing/warning rhetoric that ends in confession, land-rest, remembrance, and non-abandonment.

chapters.notes: Covenant blessings and escalating warnings, Divine dwelling, Exodus liberation, siege/exile trauma, land Sabbath, confession, covenant remembrance, non-abandonment.

translation_notes: Leviticus 26 blessing culminates in Divine dwelling and walking among the people, grounded in liberation from Egypt.
The curse section is ancient communal covenant-warning rhetoric and must not be individualized into blame for every illness, disaster, disability, poverty, or trauma.
Siege cannibalism imagery is catastrophic war trauma text, not threat theater.
The land receives its denied Sabbaths during desolation, continuing the land-rest theology of Leviticus 25.
Verses 44-45 deny total rejection and reaffirm covenant remembrance even in exile.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus 26 requires comparison with Deuteronomy covenant blessings/curses and ancient Near Eastern treaty forms.
Leviticus 26 sevenfold escalation terminology requires literary/source audit.
Leviticus 26 disease, famine, war, siege, and exile language requires anti-trauma and anti-victim-blaming reception safeguards.
Leviticus 26:29 siege cannibalism requires historical trauma and lament-text audit.
Leviticus 26 land Sabbath consequences should crosslink with Leviticus 25 and later exile narratives.
Leviticus 26 confession of ancestral iniquity requires communal-accountability framing without inherited-guilt abuse.
Leviticus 26 covenant remembrance and non-rejection should remain the interpretive endpoint.
next_actions: Finish Leviticus with chapter 27: vows and valuations, dedicated persons/animals/houses/fields, redemption plus fifth, devoted things, firstborn, and tithe.
